materialChanges: Reframed the fictional frog-audio transfer case so a local reference tone and calls traverse the same microphone-to-file path, narrowed Hypothesis 2 to whole-path attenuation, revised the prediction table, and bounded the conclusion against selective failures outside the comparison.

Revised the fictional lamp classification item so its hypothesis explicitly relates battery voltage to measured light output under unchanged controller and measurement conditions, making the concrete prediction derivable from the stated relationship.
Corrected both lesson-version references in ATTRIBUTION.md from 0.1.0 to the manifest version 0.1.1 without changing lesson.json version or status.

reviewDispositions.rationale: content/04-transfer.md now defines a reference tone played through the same microphone-to-file path as the calls, explicitly limits Hypothesis 2 to whole-path attenuation that weakens both records, revises the completed table, and explains why intact tones count against only that stated alternative while selective failures remain untested.

assessment.json item-distinguish-roles now states that, under otherwise unchanged controller and measurement conditions, lower battery voltage lowers this lamp's measured light output; the 2.5 V versus 3.0 V observation therefore follows from the stated relationship and conditions.

The finding confirms claim-before-inspection. Its scene mappings, source mapping, scope, uncertainty, and learner-visible source notes already preserve the distinction between advance specification and the debated weight of novel versus archived evidence.

The finding confirms that item-derive-prediction is logically keyed under its explicit complete-explanation and fixed calibrated-sensor conditions; its answer logic, contrary results, rubric, retry, and remediation are internally aligned.

The finding confirms that the museum-logger assessment explicitly narrows the clock-error alternative and uses an independent clock comparison to discriminate it, while the keyed conclusion avoids proof-of-causation and exhaustive-alternative claims.
The first-attempt capstone remains the novel museum-logger and independent-clock case, distinct from the taught frog-audio case, so it continues to require reconstruction rather than transcription.

The greenhouse-alarm practice already instructs the learner to treat each hypothesis as the complete explanation and validates the learner's real-device caution before returning to the stipulated comparison.
The opening already routes a learner who cannot identify the observation, comparison, and conditions in the supplied question to the preceding lesson before continuing.
item-derive-prediction already asks the learner to state the fixed sensor condition that the corresponding rubric scores, so the criterion is cued before the attempt.

The final revision preserves the verified instructional sequence, fading support, misconception repair, novel assessment transfer, untimed text-equivalent route, source order, and recovery guidance; the narrowed audio hypothesis improves logical consistency without changing those learning operations.

evidenceChecked: lesson.json preserves a four-scene semantic reading order; every scene has exactly one level-one heading, uses ordinary Markdown headings and tables, and carries its required claim-coverage declaration.

Learner tasks are operable through prose, bullets, or tables and require no color, image, sound, dragging, pointer precision, motion, speed, laboratory activity, animal contact, recording, or upload; the fictional audio evidence and calibration relationship are fully expressed in text.

The assessment is untimed and retakeable, supplies after-attempt reasoning, equivalent retry prompts, misconception-sensitive feedback, and recovery steps; no assessed operation depends on a physical or sensory-only route.

The scene sources contain no raw HTML, JavaScript, Markdown images, remote embeds, executable code, unsafe relative paths, active media, or local third-party assets; the cases contain no identifiable learner, patient, or animal data and are explicitly fictional.

Each claims-mapped scene exposes all of its mapped material claims in a same-scene source note, and every cited source ID resolves to references.json with an HTTPS source route, use record, facts-only-original-expression basis, concrete rights evidence, and dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found status.

lesson.json declares CC-BY-4.0, names the accountable author and material authoring-agent provenance, and lists no third-party assets; ATTRIBUTION.md discloses original fictional cases, no included assets or datasets, both facts-only source uses, the authoring agent, and the corrected lesson version 0.1.1.

The revised transfer case states its model boundary beside the claim: the diagnostic addresses stipulated whole-path attenuation, while selective detector or frequency-specific failures remain outside the two-hypothesis comparison.
limitations: Per the operator's explicit boundary, no npm source preflight, repository validation, rendered-browser test, screen-reader session, zoom/reflow test, or export test was run in this finalizer turn.

The site schemas and machine-readable quorum policy were outside the permitted read scope, so this artifact was checked manually against the supplied standard-lesson facts and adjudication template; the operator must run the canonical schema and repository checks after stamping the final commit.

The external source and terms pages were not reopened in this finalizer turn; the rights audit used the complete dated access records in references.json and the academic review's direct public-source checks.

GitHub COMMENTED transports and non-dismissal were supplied by the operator and were not independently queried from GitHub in this run; declared providers, models, run IDs, and principals remain attested provenance rather than platform-verified independence.

rationale: The standard-lesson review-input gate counts exactly the two operator-identified artifacts against candidate fe0bdb3d2ea49bbcca940d136b6a89334b9a8910: one academic request-changes input and one learning-design approval, from two distinct declared agent runs and two declared model families/providers under one disclosed accountable principal. The two older pack review artifacts were inspected but excluded because they target lesson version 0.1.0 and candidate 347c0bca7014b7e70de25019897f5f9f4713502b. The academic review's major transfer-logic finding and minor lamp-item finding were incorporated; its three confirming notes and all five learning-design notes require no further change. Manual recomputation of answer logic, objective and outcome links, rubric totals, claim-to-scene and claim-to-source links, source-note coverage, glossary links, file paths, semantic headings, directive fences, inert-content boundaries, attribution, and access/rights records found no remaining content blocker. The resulting lesson is accurate within its explicit simplified-model boundaries, instructionally coherent, accessible through a complete text route, and supported by a complete facts-only rights ledger. Merge is therefore the accountable content decision, subject to the recorded operator-only commit stamping and canonical validation conditions; this does not claim that the declared provenance or GitHub transport was independently verified.
dissent: The academic reviewer requested changes because the original frog-audio diagnostic did not entail that every relevant recorder failure would affect its inserted tone and because the original lamp prediction depended on an unstated light-output relation. Both objections were accepted and incorporated; the request-changes verdict remains part of the record rather than being treated as an approval of the final edit.
conditions: The operator must commit the final lesson content with both cited review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character commit SHA, and stamp adjudicator.agent system, provider, model, and version from the runtime banner; no teaching content may change after that commit.

Before merge, the operator must run the strict source preflight and canonical repository validation, including adjudication-schema, commit-order, final-content, review-transport, and GitHub-provenance checks; any failure blocks merge until handled through a separately authorized process.
The maintainer, not this finalizer, performs any permitted publication transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields after finalization.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev operating the fresh finalizer and adjudicator
